atomic_claim: A tendency-based collaborative filtering approach models user and item tendency for score prediction.
The approach incorporates text review analysis with respect to ratings.
The approach unbiases review rating predictions by accounting for personal user bias preferences.
A lightweight convolutional neural network rates AMR quality by converting AMR graphs into images.
The CNN rates AMR quality more accurately than strong baselines across multiple quality dimensions.
The CNN is computationally efficient and reduces energy consumption compared to alternative methods.
A novel unsupervised approach combines lexical rule-based methods with coreference resolution for aspect extraction.
The approach improves aspect extraction accuracy by recognizing and removing coreferring aspects.
Experimental evaluation on two benchmark datasets shows the approach outperforms baseline methods in extracting coherent aspects.
Two knowledge-transfer approaches from monolingual pretrained models to multilingual models improve zero-shot cross-lingual classification.
The knowledge-transfer approaches achieve higher performance than vanilla multilingual fine-tuning on two cross-lingual classification benchmarks.
A neural contextual utterance encoder with three types of self-attention mechanisms is introduced.
The encoder is trained using siamese and triplet energy-based meta-architectures.
The resulting system outperforms multiple energy-based and non-energy-based baselines on the AMI corpus.
An entity linking model jointly learns mention detection and entity disambiguation.
The model applies task-specific heads on top of shared BERT contextualized embeddings.
The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on a standard entity linking dataset.
The model performs well when hand-crafted entity candidate sets are unavailable.
A deep learning framework with joint inference captures both tag semantics and geographic correlation between questions and points of interest.
The framework includes a cross-attention question embedding neural network that models question-to-POI and POI-to-question interactions.
The framework utilizes a skewed distribution to simulate spatial relationships between questions and points of interest.
Empirical evaluation demonstrates that the framework is robust and practical compared to existing methods.
An unsupervised scoring function that leverages cross-lingual sentence embeddings to compute semantic distance between documents in different languages is introduced.
The semantic distances produced by the scoring function are used to guide a document alignment algorithm for pairing cross-lingual web documents across low-, mid-, and high-resource language pairs.
A tractable greedy algorithm is proposed to approximate the computationally intensive scoring function while maintaining comparable performance.
The proposed distance metric achieves higher alignment accuracy than current baselines across high-, mid-, and low-resource language pairs.
Reliance on gendered word pairs for bias measurement yields non-robust bias measures that fail to identify common real-world bias types.
Analogies constructed using gendered word pairs are unsuitable indicators of gender bias.
The analogy “man is to computer-programmer as woman is to homemaker” reflects word similarity rather than gender bias.
A general framework adapts neural machine translation for simultaneous translation.
The framework includes a prefix translation component that uses a consecutive NMT model to translate source prefixes.
The framework includes a stopping criterion that determines when to stop prefix translation.
Experiments on three translation corpora and two language pairs demonstrate that the framework balances translation quality and latency in simultaneous translation.
BCTH utilizes multiple Bayesian clusterings in parallel to map documents to binary codes, assigning one bit to each clustering.
BCTH incorporates a bit-balanced constraint to maximize the information content of each bit.
BCTH incorporates a bit-uncorrected constraint to maintain independence among all bits.
BCTH learns hash codes and hash functions jointly with linear time complexity.
BCTH achieves competitive precision and training speed compared to existing baselines.
BCTH represents the first application of Bayesian clustering to text hashing.
Length prediction is introduced as an auxiliary task for neural machine translation.
A sub-network extracts target length information from the encoder.
The length prediction sub-network improves translation performance over a strong baseline.
Predicted target length can replace length normalization during decoding.
A BERT-based method classifies MRC training examples as answerable or unanswerable when only the context is provided.
The method partitions the SQuAD1.1 training set into "easy to answer" examples that are answerable without a question and "hard to answer" examples that are not.
Training an MRC model on the "hard to answer" subset outperforms training on the "easy to answer" subset.
Classification models that use seven numerical and ten text-based features extracted from simple Twitter profile information are introduced for identifying student users.
The profile-based models classify students without requiring access to their tweets.
The proposed models achieve higher accuracy and F1 score than prior models that rely on tweet-content features.
metaCAT is a web-based annotation tool designed for developing task-oriented dialogue data.
metaCAT provides comprehensive metadata annotation coverage for domain, intent, and span information.
metaCAT enhances data annotation quality with a real-time annotation constraint-checking mechanism.
metaCAT includes an Automatic Speech Recognition function that enables paraphrasing and creation of diversified annotated utterances.
Re-evaluation of RumourEval 2017 and 2019 systems shows that accuracy and macro-F1 are not robust for four-class imbalanced rumour stance classification.
New evaluation metrics for rumour stance detection are introduced that are robust to imbalanced data and reward recognition of the support and deny classes.
A touch-based editing method enables flexible translation post-editing using user-performed touch actions.
A dual-encoder model processes touch actions and generates refined translations.
A training data construction pipeline uses TER comparisons between draft translations and references to automatically extract simulated editing actions.
The touch-based editing method improves translation quality compared to baseline Transformer models.
The touch-based editing method outperforms existing interactive translation methods.
Experiments on post-editing datasets demonstrate the robustness of the touch-based editing method.
A two-stage compression method first decomposes model matrices into smaller matrices and then applies feature distillation on internal representations to recover information.
The compression method reduces the number of parameters of BERT-base while preserving most of the model's information.
The compression method increases inference speed of BERT-base with minimal loss in metric performance on the GLUE benchmark.
Aspect-level sentiment controllable style transfer is defined as a task where each aspect-level sentiment can be individually controlled in the generated text.
A BERT-based encoder-decoder architecture with saliency weighted polarity injection is proposed for aspect-level sentiment controllable style transfer.
Unsupervised training strategies, including ABSA masked-language-modelling, are employed to train the proposed architecture.
The system is demonstrated to successfully control aspect-level sentiments according to automatic and manual evaluation.
A task-agnostic model for estimating problem difficulty in supervised learning tasks is developed.
Regression and classification models are trained to predict how many annotators will answer a question correctly on the SNLI dataset.
Difficulty estimates from the model are projected onto the full SNLI training set to create a curriculum.
Analyses demonstrate that the curriculum captures difficulty for the SNLI task.
A survey categorizes explanations for natural language processing models.
A survey details operations and explainability techniques for generating explanations of NLP model predictions.
A survey identifies current gaps in explainable AI for natural language processing.
An automatic web-based technique combines rule-based identification, informatics-based extraction, and Baidu search engine validation to identify Mandarin Alphabetical Words from Sina Weibo.
The technique produces a large collection of Mandarin Alphabetical Words sourced from Sina Weibo.
An annotated corpus of hundreds of thousands of sentences is released for linguistic research.
Two methods that integrate a BERT-based pre-trained model into an encoder-decoder architecture improve Chinese grammatical error correction.
Error analysis shows that sentence-level errors are still insufficiently addressed in Chinese grammatical error correction.
Re-annotated Indonesian NER dataset yields more consistent organization tag annotations.
Using BiLSTM-CRF, the re-annotated dataset improves NER prediction performance compared to the original annotation.
Comparative analysis of feature embeddings reveals their impact on Indonesian NER task performance.
SportsSum is a Chinese sports game summarization dataset containing 5,428 soccer games with live commentaries and corresponding news articles.
A two-step summarization model composed of a selector and a rewriter is introduced for sports game summarization.
Name matching score and event matching score are introduced as novel metrics for evaluating the correctness of generated sports summaries.
The two-step model outperforms existing summarization baselines on ROUGE and the proposed name and event matching scores.
VScript generates complete scripts with dialogues and scene descriptions and provides visual presentation via video retrieval.
VScript allows users to control script generation by selecting genres and providing starting words.
VScript employs a hierarchical generation process that first creates a plot, then generates the script and its visual presentation.
Plot-guided dialogue generation is performed by treating dialogue generation as inverse dialogue summarization.
VScript outperforms baseline methods on automatic and human evaluations, particularly in genre control.
A single convolutional neural network with sinusoidal positional encoding is introduced for text classification.
Sinusoidal positional encoding supplies word position information that enables a more efficient CNN architecture for text classification.
The model reduces both parameter count and training time compared to prior CNN-based text classifiers while preserving comparable performance on multiple benchmark datasets.
A supervised topic model is incorporated to provide global context that improves event trigger detection.
A preprocessing module discards unlikely candidate antecedents of event mentions using discourse contexts derived from salient entities.
The extended joint model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the KBP 2017 English and Chinese event coreference datasets.
A method predicts salient locations from news article text using a knowledge base.
The method uses a dictionary of locations derived from the knowledge base to identify location mentions in text.
The method leverages hierarchical information between entities in the knowledge base to assign saliency scores to regions.
The method supports prediction at arbitrary region units.
The method requires only a few hyperparameters to be tuned.
The method achieves higher f-measure than multiple baselines on manually annotated news articles.
AMesure assists writers of French administrative texts in simplifying their writing.
AMesure includes a readability formula specialized for administrative texts.
AMesure uses various NLP tools to analyze texts and highlight linguistic phenomena considered difficult to read.
AMesure offers pieces of advice from official plain language guides based on identified difficulties.
Components of AMesure are evaluated.
A high-order refining mechanism performs interaction between all predicate-argument pairs in end-to-end Chinese semantic role labeling.
The high-order refining module learns higher-order features between candidate predicate-argument pairs via attention calculation.
Learned higher-order features are used to update token representations.
Iterative refinement enriches token representations with globally interacted features.
The high-order model achieves state-of-the-art performance on Chinese semantic role labeling datasets CoNLL09 and Universal Proposition Bank.
The high-order model alleviates long-range dependency issues in Chinese semantic role labeling.
Grammar induction models that incorporate visual information from images for labeled parsing are introduced.
The introduced models achieve state-of-the-art performance on grounded grammar induction across multiple languages.
Visual information is particularly beneficial in languages where high-frequency words have broader distribution.
Grounded models leverage visual information to propose noun phrases.
Grounded models leverage visual information to gather useful information for unknown words.
Grounded models achieve improved prepositional phrase attachment prediction by using visual information.
An empirical evaluation of multiple tokenization strategies across Korean NLP tasks is performed.

A hybrid tokenization approach that applies morphological segmentation followed by BPE achieves the best performance for Korean↔English machine translation and Korean natural language understanding tasks such as KorNLI, KorSTS, NSMC, and PAWS-X.
BPE tokenization alone provides the most effective performance for the Korean question answering dataset KorQuAD.
A fully unsupervised parsing approach extracts constituency trees from transformer attention heads.
The approach ranks transformer attention heads based on inherent properties and ensembles high-ranking heads to produce constituency trees.
The approach does not require development sets, making it adaptable to low-resource languages.
Experiments show the unsupervised parser often outperforms existing approaches when no development set is available.
Parse trees induced by the unsupervised parser can be used to train a neural PCFG for comparison with grammars derived from human-annotated treebanks.
Video Timeline Tags (ViTT) is a dense video captioning dataset containing instructional videos with time-stamped annotations.
Several multimodal sequence-to-sequence pretraining strategies that leverage large unsupervised video and caption-like text datasets are proposed for dense video captioning.
Pretraining and finetuning dense video captioning models on YouCook2 and ViTT yields models that generalize well and are robust across diverse instructional videos.
A deep attentive multi-task learning framework jointly performs humour detection, sarcasm detection, offensive content detection, motivational content detection, and sentiment analysis on memes.
The framework incorporates an Inter-task Relationship Module that learns relationships between the tasks.
The framework incorporates an Inter-class Relationship Module that learns relationships between the classes of tasks.
Representations from the Inter-task and Inter-class Relationship Modules are concatenated and shared across the five meme analysis tasks.
Empirical evaluation on the Memotion Analysis dataset shows that the multi-task framework outperforms existing state-of-the-art systems.
Empirical evaluation indicates that the multi-task framework achieves better performance than single-task learning.
Second-order graph-based neural dependency parsing with message passing and end-to-end training is introduced.
The proposed second-order parsing approaches achieve accuracy comparable to recent state-of-the-art second-order graph-based neural dependency parsers.
The proposed approaches achieve significantly faster training and testing speed than recent state-of-the-art second-order graph-based neural dependency parsers.
Second-order parsing shows an advantage over first-order parsing.
The usefulness of the head-selection structured constraint vanishes when using BERT embeddings.
A novel task is defined to explain evidences in hotel recommendations given vague natural language requests.

The task is decomposed into Evidence Identification, which predicts whether a sentence contains evidence satisfying a request, and Evidence Explanation, which generates a recommendation sentence from a request and an evidence sentence.
An Evidence-based Explanation dataset is constructed, representing the largest dataset for explaining evidences in hotel recommendations for vague requests.
A BERT model is shown to identify evidence sentences relevant to various vague requests.
An LSTM-based model is shown to generate recommendation sentences conditioned on a request and an evidence sentence.
A hierarchical attention BiLSTM neural topic segmenter is enhanced with a coherence-related auxiliary task and restricted self-attention to improve context modeling.
The enhanced segmenter outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on three datasets.
The enhanced segmenter demonstrates robustness in domain transfer when trained on a large-scale dataset and evaluated on four real-world benchmarks.
The proposed context modeling strategy is effective for neural topic segmentation in German.
The proposed context modeling strategy is effective for neural topic segmentation in Chinese.
A method transforms dense word vectors into disentangled embeddings that encode polysemous semantics separately.
Disentangled embeddings provide improved interpretability of word representations.
The modular structure of disentangled word embeddings generates more efficient and effective features for natural language processing tasks.
A WordNet-enhanced hierarchical model (WEHM) for answer selection incorporates synset and hypernym concepts into word representations.
WEHM integrates similarity scores of synset or hypernym related concepts into its attention mechanism.
WEHM consists of four modules: WordNet-enhanced word representation, sentence encoding, WordNet-enhanced attention mechanism, and hierarchical document encoding.
WEHM achieves performance improvements over baseline systems on the WikiQA and SelQA datasets.
WEHM outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on the WikiQA and SelQA datasets.
An unsupervised KB-to-text generation method trains on unaligned raw text data and KB triples.
A dual training framework leverages the inverse relationship between KB-to-text generation and auxiliary triple extraction.
The architecture links a generator and an extractor in a closed-loop to reconstruct KB triples or texts, using reconstruction error as training loss.
A pseudo data generator creates pseudo texts and KB triples to initialize model training.
An allocated reinforcement learning component optimizes reconstruction loss to address the multiple-triple problem.
The model outperforms other unsupervised generation methods and approaches the performance of supervised methods.
A hierarchical neural network jointly predicts document-level holistic essay scores and word- and sentence-level sentiment classes via multi-task learning.
Sentiment features extracted from opinion expressions are incorporated to enhance holistic essay scoring.
Sentiment features are beneficial for some essay prompts.
The approach achieves competitive performance with other deep learning models on the Automated Student Assessment Prize benchmark.
The model attains strong agreement with human graders as measured by Quadratic Weighted Kappa.
Incorporating silver-standard RST features increases accuracy in discourse coherence classification.
RST-Recursive is a tree-recursive neural model that leverages RST features from a state-of-the-art RST parser for discourse coherence evaluation.
Ensembling RST-Recursive with the current state-of-the-art yields new state-of-the-art accuracy on the Grammarly Corpus for Discourse Coherence benchmark.
RST-Recursive alone achieves competitive accuracy on the Grammarly Corpus for Discourse Coherence benchmark while using substantially fewer parameters.
A multi-view classification model evaluates candidate triples for knowledge graph completion using both content information within the triple and context information surrounding the triple.
Each classification view in the model is implemented with a unified neural network.
Classification predictions from multiple views are combined through weighted integration to produce a final evaluation of candidate triples.
The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on two representative knowledge graph completion datasets.
The model’s flexibility and scalability enable incorporation of additional information and resources to improve performance.
ExpanRL is an end-to-end hierarchical reinforcement learning model for concept expansion in MOOCs.
ExpanRL employs a two-level hierarchical reinforcement learning mechanism comprising seed selection and concept expansion.
ExpanRL adjusts the expansion strategy based on student feedback on expansion results.
Nine novel datasets from real MOOCs are introduced for evaluating concept expansion.
ExpanRL outperforms existing methods and maintains competitive performance across different settings.
MaintNet is an open-source library and data repository that provides predictive maintenance language datasets.
Novel annotated datasets for predictive maintenance are provided for aviation, automotive, and facility maintenance domains.
New tools for segmentation, spell checking, part-of-speech tagging, clustering, and classification of maintenance logbook records are introduced.
TexPrax is a messaging system that enables collection and annotation of problems, causes, and solutions in work-related chats.
TexPrax employs a chatbot to engage employees in providing lightweight annotations on their conversations.
TexPrax incorporates end-to-end message encryption and gives users full control over their data, providing advantages over conventional annotation tools.
TexPrax was evaluated in a user study with German factory employees.
TexPrax produced a dataset of task-oriented German dialogues with sentence-level expert annotations.
Analysis of the collected dialogues reveals frequent code-switching, varying abbreviations for the same entity, and dialectal variations.
SQuARE v2 provides an explainability infrastructure that supports comparing QA models using saliency maps and graph-based explanations.
Graph-based explanations derived from external Knowledge Graphs enable users to verify the reasoning behind QA model predictions.
SQuARE v2 includes multiple adversarial attacks for evaluating the robustness of QA models.
A comparative evaluation of probabilistic, distributional, and Transformer-based models for logical metonymy interpretation is presented.
BERT, RoBERTa, XLNet, and GPT-2 are evaluated on logical metonymy interpretation for the first time.
Best Transformer-based models achieve performance comparable to top traditional distributional models on logical metonymy interpretation.
Performance remains low on certain testing datasets, indicating that logical metonymy remains challenging for computational modeling.
A siamese convolutional neural network architecture learns a semantic similarity metric between two sentences.
Cosine similarity and Manhattan similarity are compared as similarity metrics within the siamese convolutional neural network architecture.

The siamese convolutional neural network architecture with Manhattan similarity outperforms siamese LSTM and siamese bidirectional LSTM architectures on binary Chinese sentence similarity classification.
A graph attention network with memory fusion is introduced for aspect-level sentiment analysis.
The network assigns different weights to edges in the graph, unlike graph convolutional networks that assign equal weight.
Syntactic constraints are imposed to block graph convolutional propagation of words unrelated to the target aspect.
A convolutional layer and memory fusion are applied to learn and exploit multiword relations and assign varying weights to words.
Experimental evaluation on multiple benchmark datasets shows the method outperforms existing approaches.
A dataset of location trajectories of 729 students over seven months together with textual data related to those locations is introduced.
Several strategies for creating embeddings of spatial locations from trajectories and textual representations are implemented.
Embeddings of sequences of visited locations are generated using the location embeddings.

Probing tasks are proposed to assess surface-level properties captured by location embeddings, such as the presence of a specific location in a sequence or the type of activities occurring at a location.
Location embeddings are employed to predict a student's area of study.
Location embeddings are employed to predict a student's depression level.
A suite of pretrained intent detection models trained on wikiHow is introduced.
The models can predict a broad range of intended goals from many actions.
The models achieve state-of-the-art results on the Snips dataset, the Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset, and the Facebook multilingual dialog datasets.
The models demonstrate strong zero- and few-shot performance with limited training data.
The syntax of primary statements for the Sanskritam programming language, based on natural Sanskrit, is formally specified.
Six primary statement forms—declaration, assignment, inline initialization, if‑then‑else, for loop, and while loop—are defined for Sanskritam.
The specification incorporates techniques from Sanskrit textual traditions such as sūtra‑based disciplines and Navya‑Nyāya formal language into modern Sanskrit NLP.
A modality-transferable model uses emotion embeddings for multimodal emotion recognition.
Pre-trained word embeddings represent emotion categories for textual data in the model.
Two mapping functions are learned to transfer emotion embeddings into visual and acoustic spaces.
The model predicts emotions by calculating representation distances between input sequences and target emotion embeddings for each modality.
The model can directly adapt to unseen emotions in any modality using pre-trained embeddings and modality mapping functions.
The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on most emotion categories.
The model outperforms existing baselines in zero-shot and few-shot scenarios for unseen emotions.
JS divergence is used to detect changes in the attention mode during BERT fine-tuning.
SVCCA distance is used to examine changes in the feature extraction mode during BERT fine-tuning.
BERT fine-tuning primarily changes the attention mode of the last layers.
BERT fine-tuning modifies the feature extraction mode of the intermediate and last layers.
The consistency of BERT fine-tuning is analyzed across different random seeds and datasets.
A systematic exploration and comparison of different redundancy reduction strategies for summarizing long documents is performed.
Existing redundancy reduction methods are organized into categories based on when and how redundancy is considered.
Three new methods that balance non-redundancy and importance in a general and flexible manner are introduced.
The introduced methods achieve state-of-the-art ROUGE performance on PubMed and arXiv scientific paper summarization datasets while significantly reducing redundancy.
A multi-task learning framework learns gaze behavior at run time to improve automatic essay grading.
The framework improves performance over the state-of-the-art on essay sets with available gaze data.
The framework improves performance on essay sets without any gaze data.
A sentiment-aware news encoder is jointly trained with an auxiliary sentiment prediction task to produce sentiment-aware news representations.
A sentiment diversity regularization method penalizes the model by combining the overall sentiment orientation of browsed news with the click and sentiment scores of candidate news.
User representations are derived from browsed news representations and combined with candidate news representations to compute click scores.
The proposed approach improves sentiment diversity in news recommendation without sacrificing recommendation performance.
Multilingual Quality Estimation approaches that incorporate translation model scores outperform single-language QE models.
The performance advantage of multilingual QE is especially pronounced for imbalanced quality label distributions and low-resource language settings.
Zero-shot Quality Estimation can accurately predict translation quality for a previously unseen language using models trained on other languages.
Neural QE models built on powerful pre-trained representations generalize effectively across multiple languages.
DAPPER learns domain-adapted persona representations by combining a pretrained language model with an external memory.
DAPPER embeds persona from natural language to alleviate task- or domain-specific data sparsity.
Transferability of DAPPER's persona embeddings is evaluated in simulated low-resource scenarios.
DAPPER's persona embeddings are more transferable than those produced by other approaches.
Learned persona embeddings improve performance on downstream tasks such as hate speech detection.
Word clusters are incorporated into the Continuous Bag-of-Words model by replacing infrequent input and output words with their clusters.
The resulting cluster-incorporated CBOW model generates embeddings for frequent words and a limited set of cluster embeddings that can be fine-tuned in downstream tasks.
The word-cluster replacement method achieves effective performance on multiple downstream tasks.
Analysis indicates that the word-cluster replacement method may be applicable to other models that generate word embeddings.
Label smoothing optimizes a regularized objective that penalizes over‑confident outputs in neural machine translation.
Varying the set of tokens to which label smoothing is applied influences neural machine translation performance.
Tuning the probability mass removed from true target tokens and choosing different prior distributions can further improve the performance of strong neural machine translation systems.
Theoretical analysis demonstrates that label smoothing is well‑motivated for neural machine translation.
Bridge concepts are extracted from multi-hop reasoning paths and integrated to generate commonsense explanations.
External commonsense knowledge is used to construct a subgraph of multi-hop paths that connect a statement to bridge concepts.
A bridge concept extraction model scores triples, routes paths in the subgraph, and selects bridge concepts with weak supervision at both the triple and concept levels.
The proposed method achieves superior performance compared to prior baselines on commonsense explanation generation in both automatic and human evaluations.
Unsupervised extractive summarization methods are applied to generate chapter summaries for grade 10 Hindi history textbooks.
TextRank, LexRank, Luhn, and KLSum are evaluated as extractive summarization algorithms for Hindi history chapter summarization.
Luhn and TextRank achieve the highest ROUGE scores in intrinsic evaluation of Hindi history chapter summaries.
TextRank yields the most effective summaries for answering exam questions in extrinsic evaluation.
An efficient method detects time-series performance degradation of word embeddings and pre-trained language models by calculating semantic shift.
The method enables monitoring performance to inform decisions about model re-training.
Experiments show that the method identifies time-series performance degradation in Japanese and English datasets.
Mixed-lingual pre-training combines cross-lingual translation tasks with monolingual masked language modeling tasks.
The architecture lacks task-specific components, which reduces memory consumption and improves optimization efficiency.
Mixed-lingual pre-training boosts the performance of cross-lingual summarization.
The model surpasses state-of-the-art performance on the NCLS dataset for English-to-Chinese and Chinese-to-English cross-lingual summarization.
A joint top-down framework learns from both discourse dependency and constituency parsing using a shared encoder and two independent decoders.
A constituency-to-dependency conversion scheme tailored for the Chinese discourse corpus enables high-quality joint learning of discourse parsing.
Incorporating dependency information enhances understanding of rhetorical structure, especially for upper-level tree layers, in discourse parsing.
fairseq S2T is a fairseq extension for speech-to-text modeling tasks such as end-to-end speech recognition and speech-to-text translation.
fairseq S2T provides end-to-end workflows covering data pre-processing, model training, and offline (online) inference.
fairseq S2T implements state-of-the-art RNN-based and Transformer-based models for speech-to-text tasks.
fairseq S2T enables seamless integration of machine translation and language models into speech-to-text workflows for multi-task learning or transfer learning.
Two-Headed Monster (THM) introduces two symmetric encoder modules and one decoder module connected with co-attention for neural transduction models.
Crossed Co-Attention Networks (CCNs) implement the THM paradigm using a Transformer-based architecture.
Crossed Co-Attention Networks achieve higher BLEU scores than a strong Transformer baseline on EN-DE and EN-FI translation tasks.
Crossed Co-Attention Networks increase training epoch time by approximately 75% relative to the Transformer baseline.

IndoNLU provides a benchmark comprising twelve Indonesian natural language understanding tasks spanning single-sentence classification to pair-sentence sequence labeling across diverse domains and styles.
Indo4B is a large clean Indonesian dataset compiled from publicly available sources such as social media texts, blogs, news, and websites.
IndoBERT is a set of Indonesian pre-trained language models trained on the Indo4B dataset.
Baseline models are released for all twelve IndoNLU tasks.
A framework for benchmark evaluation of Indonesian natural language understanding systems is released.
CrowdChecked provides a large-scale collection of 330,000 tweets paired with corresponding fact-checking articles.
An end-to-end framework employing modified self-adaptive training learns from noisy crowd-sourced tweet–article pairs in a distant supervision scenario.
The framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on the CLEF’21 CheckThat! test set.
An alternative training strategy that explicitly incorporates utterance frequency improves model robustness to distribution shifts between typical NLU data and real-world data.
A methodology is introduced to simulate utterance usage and create head, body, and tail segments in public NLU corpora.
Two domain generalization approaches are adapted for named entity recognition.
The proposed approaches increase semantic accuracy on tail data compared to baseline methods.
Analysis reveals that the reasons for observed improvements differ from those reported in previous work.
Attentive Variational State Tracker (AVAST) approximates belief state distribution for a reinforced navigator using a variational approach.
A fine-tuning process using policy optimization stabilizes the learning procedure for the reinforced navigator.
AVAST improves generalization in vision-and-language navigation tasks compared to previous methods.
AVAST achieves significant performance in unseen environments without requiring additional exploration.
Ancestral sampling algorithms for auto-regressive language models are characterized using the quality-diversity trade-off.
Entropy reduction, order preservation, and slope preservation are identified as key properties shared among top-k, nucleus, and tempered sampling.
A set of new sampling methods is introduced that satisfies entropy reduction, order preservation, and slope preservation.
Another set of new sampling methods is introduced that violates at least one of entropy reduction, order preservation, or slope preservation.
Violating any of the identified properties leads to drastic performance degradation in open-ended language generation as measured by the quality-diversity trade-off.
Sampling methods that satisfy all three identified properties achieve performance comparable to existing sampling algorithms.
Director is a unified generator-classifier architecture that adds a classification head to each output token alongside the language modeling head.
Director is trained jointly on standard language modeling data and data labeled with desirable and undesirable sequences.
Director achieves competitive training and decoding speed relative to standard language models while avoiding toxic, repetitive, and contradictory generations and maintaining generation quality.
Director outperforms existing model guiding approaches in both accuracy and efficiency.
Missing Modality-based Meta Sampling (M3S) is a meta-sampling approach for multimodal sentiment analysis with missing modalities.
M3S formulates a missing-modality sampling strategy within the modal-agnostic meta-learning (MAML) framework.
M3S can be applied as an efficient add-on training component to existing multimodal sentiment analysis models.
M3S improves performance of multimodal sentiment analysis models on data with a mixture of missing modalities.
Experiments on IEMOCAP, SIMS, and CMU-MOSI demonstrate that M3S achieves superior performance compared with recent state-of-the-art methods.
A novel training strategy models flexible relative proximity of neighbors retrieved from a resource-rich corpus to learn the amount of knowledge transfer for hate speech detection.
Neighborhood information is incorporated with Optimal Transport to exploit the geometry of the data embedding space in hate speech detection.
Aligning joint embedding and label distributions of neighbors improves low-resource hate speech detection performance over strong baselines.
Resources for fairness evaluation in the Indian context are built.
Prediction biases along prominent axes of social disparities in India are demonstrated using the built resources.
Social stereotypes for region and religion are shown to be prevalent in corpora and models.
A holistic research agenda to re-contextualize NLP fairness research for the Indian context is outlined.
The proposed framework for re-contextualizing NLP fairness can be generalized to other geo-cultural contexts.

A knowledge-enhanced method for named entity disambiguation incorporates factual and conceptual knowledge graphs to improve semantic matching between candidate entities and mention contexts in short text.
Factual and conceptual knowledge graphs are introduced to provide additional knowledge that addresses information ambiguity and incompleteness in short-text named entity disambiguation.
The method achieves state-of-the-art performance on three standard named entity disambiguation datasets.
The method achieves significant improvement over previous methods on a large manually annotated short-text dataset.
A large manually annotated short-text dataset for named entity disambiguation is released for research use.
ProtoPatient combines prototypical networks with label-wise attention to enable interpretable diagnosis prediction from clinical text.
ProtoPatient makes predictions by identifying text segments similar to prototypical patients, thereby providing justifications understandable to clinicians.
ProtoPatient outperforms existing baselines on two publicly available clinical diagnosis prediction datasets.
Quantitative and qualitative evaluations with medical doctors demonstrate that ProtoPatient provides valuable explanations for clinical decision support.
RecInDial integrates recommendation into dialog generation using a vocabulary pointer.
RecInDial incorporates a knowledge-aware bias learned from an entity-oriented knowledge graph to improve recommendation performance.
RecInDial fine-tunes large-scale pretrained language models to generate fluent and diverse conversational responses.
RecInDial introduces an end-to-end evaluation protocol for conversational recommender systems that assesses overall system performance.
RecInDial achieves state-of-the-art performance on the ReDial benchmark dataset.
Cognitive Knowledge Graph (CogKG) unifies expert rules and relational facts into a single representation of heterogeneous symbolic knowledge.
A general framework enables unified knowledge representations to perform both learning and reasoning.
When no labeled data are available, the framework utilizes symbolic knowledge directly as the decision basis for reasoning.
When labeled data are available, the framework casts symbolic knowledge as a trainable neural architecture optimized via gradient descent.
Empirical evaluation on two clinical diagnosis benchmarks shows that the proposed method outperforms traditional knowledge-driven and data-driven methods.
Bag-of-Vectors Autoencoders encode text into a variable-size bag of vectors that grows with text length, enabling encoding and reconstruction of much longer texts than standard autoencoders.
Regularization techniques are introduced to facilitate learning meaningful operations in the latent space of Bag-of-Vectors Autoencoders.
A training scheme with a novel loss function and neural architecture is adapted to map an input bag of vectors to an output bag of vectors.
Empirical evaluations on unsupervised sentiment transfer show that the proposed method outperforms a standard autoencoder.
A shared-private multitask architecture based on transfer learning is designed for humor detection across multiple humor forms.
The architecture is evaluated on four gold standard humor detection datasets.
The evaluation demonstrates statistically significant improvements over baseline methods.
The evaluation achieves new state-of-the-art performance on two of the datasets.
A graph neural network is used to inject explicit tabular structures into a financial question answering model.
A GNN-based evidence extraction module for tables is added to the baseline financial QA model.
An improved numerical reasoning module that handles arithmetic operations requiring number ordering, such as subtraction and division, is incorporated into the model.
The graph module provides performance advantages in low-resource settings for financial question answering.
The improved numerical reasoning module achieves higher performance than the baseline model on financial question answering.
fastcoref is a pip-installable Python package for English coreference resolution that is fast, accurate, and easy to use.
fastcoref provides an accurate mode based on the LingMess architecture that achieves state-of-the-art coreference accuracy.

fastcoref provides a faster model, F-coref, that processes OntoNotes documents in seconds on a V100 GPU, substantially faster than the LingMess model and the AllenNLP coreference model, with only a modest drop in accuracy.
The speed of F-coref is achieved through distillation of a compact model from LingMess and an efficient batching implementation called leftover batching.
NGEP generates event sequences by performing inference on an automatically constructed event graph.
NGEP enhances generalisation ability through a neural event advisor.
NGEP outperforms state-of-the-art event planning approaches on event sequence generation.
NGEP outperforms state-of-the-art event planning approaches on downstream story generation.
An automated unsupervised approach selects dynamic-size and potentially non-contiguous citation contexts using transformer-based document representations and embedding similarities.
Adding non-contiguous citing sentences to citation context improves citation intent classification performance beyond previous results.
Including additional context beyond the citing sentence significantly improves citation classification model performance across both domain-specific and multi-disciplinary datasets.
Text Characterization Toolkit (TCT) enables study of characteristics of large datasets.
Text Characterization Toolkit (TCT) provides an easy-to-use interface and off-the-shelf scripts for specific dataset analyses.
Text Characterization Toolkit (TCT) facilitates deeper analysis of datasets at scale for training, development, and evaluation.
Text Characterization Toolkit (TCT) predicts difficult examples for given trained models.
Text Characterization Toolkit (TCT) identifies potentially harmful biases present in datasets.
A novel architecture uses discrete latent variables to represent dialogue actions for explainable modeling of task-oriented dialogues.
The architecture is built on variational recurrent neural networks and does not require explicit semantic annotation.

The architecture models system and user turns separately and incorporates database query modeling, enabling applicability to task-oriented dialogues while yielding interpretable action latent variables.
The model achieves superior performance with less supervision compared to previous approaches on three datasets.
A method is introduced to measure dialogue success without expert annotation.
A technique is introduced to explain the semantics of latent variables with respect to system actions.
Fine-tuning with Prompt Curriculum (FPC) is a relation extraction method that incorporates relation prompt learning to capture the semantics of relation labels.
Fine-tuning with Prompt Curriculum (FPC) employs a prompt learning curriculum that progressively increases task difficulty to adapt the model to a multi-task setting.
Fine-tuning with Prompt Curriculum (FPC) achieves state-of-the-art performance on four widely used relation extraction benchmarks in both fully supervised and low-resource settings.
PInKS is a model that performs preconditioned commonsense inference with weak supervision.
PInKS attains higher Macro-F1 performance on benchmarks evaluating reasoning with preconditions of commonsense knowledge.
PAC-Bayesian informativeness analysis is used to evaluate the informativeness of PInKS.
Precision measures and an ablation study are conducted to analyze the components of PInKS.
A conceptual framework for addressing implicitness, ambiguity, and underspecification in scene description-to-depiction tasks is presented.
Generating visually ambiguous images is proposed as a strategy to mitigate textual and visual ambiguity in scene description-to-depiction systems.
Generating a set of diverse images is proposed as a strategy to mitigate textual and visual ambiguity in scene description-to-depiction systems.
VQA models exhibit performance discrepancies when evaluated on the standard VQA‑v2 dataset versus the accessibility‑focused VizWiz dataset.
A comparative analysis of VQA model behavior across VQA‑v2 and VizWiz highlights specific challenges and opportunities for deploying VQA in accessibility applications.
SummVD performs unsupervised extractive summarization using singular value decomposition to represent words in a low-dimensional hidden-topic space.
SummVD applies word clustering to reduce vocabulary size.
SummVD employs a linear sentence extraction heuristic for summary generation.
SummVD outperforms recent extractive summarization approaches in effectiveness.
SummVD requires low computational and data resources, enabling fast summarization of long single documents and large multi-document corpora for live systems.
GenTyDiQA provides well-formed complete answers for Arabic, Bengali, English, Japanese, and Russian as an extension of the TyDiQA dataset.
A cross-lingual generative model produces full-sentence answers by leveraging passages in multiple languages, including languages different from the question.
The cross-lingual generative system outperforms answer sentence selection baselines across five languages and surpasses monolingual generative pipelines for three of those languages.
Open-domain generative question answering is extended to multilingual and cross-lingual settings.
PoS tags are generally more useful for sequence labeling parsers than for other parsing paradigms.
The impact of PoS tag accuracy on sequence labeling parsers is highly dependent on the encoding method.
PoS-based head-selection encoding achieves the best performance only when both tagging accuracy and resource availability are high.
The method utilizes arc-polynomial score functions, a general framework that includes common higher-order score functions and introduces new ones.
Coordinate ascent and genetic search are used to iteratively update candidate parses through gradient-based operations.
Gradient-based updates are efficiently computed using auto-differentiation libraries.
The method achieves performance comparable to recent state-of-the-art second-order parsers on three standard datasets.
A subgraph partition algorithm partitions retrieved knowledge subgraphs into smaller sub‑KSGs.
A graph‑augmented learning‑to‑rank model selects top‑ranked sub‑KSGs.
The model incorporates subgraph matching networks that capture global interactions between the question and subgraphs.
The model incorporates an Enhanced Bilateral Multi‑Perspective Matching component that captures local interactions.
Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that the graph‑augmented learning‑to‑rank method improves answer selection performance.
NEmo+ expands the BU-NEmo dataset to include a larger collection of news headline and lead image pairings with extensive crowdsourced emotion annotations.
Methods are introduced to identify news items that trigger similar versus divergent emotional responses.
NEmo+-Consensus compiles news items that elicit similar emotional responses.
Benchmark models predict a person's dominant emotional response toward a target news item as a single-label prediction on the NEmo+ dataset.
Benchmark models predict the distribution of evoked emotional responses in humans for multi-modal news content as a multi-label prediction on the full NEmo+ dataset.
The single-label and multi-label prediction models achieve performance superior to baselines across multiple evaluation metrics.
Three legal case summarization datasets were developed.
Extensive experiments compare extractive and abstractive (supervised and unsupervised) summarization methods on the three legal case summarization datasets.
Evaluation by law practitioners provides insights into legal summarization and long document summarization.
A new challenge set for SPARQL-to-text question generation is introduced.
Four knowledge-based QA corpora are homogenized to create a unified dataset for SPARQL-to-text question generation.
The impact of different training setups on SPARQL-to-text question generation is analyzed using pretrained T5 and BART models.
Simple questions and frequent SPARQL query templates are processed well by current pretrained T5 and BART models.
Complex questions and conversational phenomena such as coreferences and ellipses remain difficult for current pretrained T5 and BART models.
A knowledge representation learning method for food knowledge graphs encodes entities and relations with a pre-trained language model to incorporate contextual information.
The method is trained on masked entity prediction and triple plausibility prediction tasks.
A set of adversarial sample generation strategies for food substitutions is introduced to evaluate the learned food knowledge graph embeddings.
Multiple strategies are introduced to generate context-aware recipe and ingredient substitutions and to provide generalized ingredient substitutions for diverse user needs.
Extensive evaluations on a large-scale food knowledge graph demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of the knowledge graph learning method and the adversarial substitution strategies.
Eye-tracking data from speakers of 13 languages reading in their native language and in English are analyzed to assess alignment between human fixation patterns and Transformer attention.
Alignment between human fixation patterns and Transformer attention differs considerably across languages.
Individual reading behaviors such as skipping rate, total reading time, and vocabulary knowledge modulate the alignment between humans and models.
An annotated dataset of 10,000 Bengali social media posts, comprising 5,000 posts in native script and 5,000 Romanized Bengali posts, is created.
Baseline classification models are implemented for detecting hateful and offensive content in Bengali posts.
An interlingual transfer mechanism is explored to improve classification performance on Bengali hate speech detection.
An error analysis is conducted on misclassified posts to investigate model failures.
XLM-Roberta achieves the highest performance when training on native script and Romanized Bengali datasets separately.
MuRIL outperforms other models in joint training and few-shot training settings by better interpreting semantic expressions.

Knowledge Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Transformer (KRAMT) treats named visual entities in an image as a gateway to encyclopedic knowledge and leverages them with natural language queries to ground relevant knowledge.
KRAMT integrates visual content and grounded knowledge to learn alignment between images and search queries.
KRAMT enables image search that requires commonsense and factual reasoning.
COFAR dataset is introduced for evaluating image search with commonsense and factual reasoning.
Retrieval performance of KRAMT is evaluated and compared with related approaches on the COFAR dataset.
An adversarial example generation method based on confusion sets creates training data for neural machine translation models to improve robustness to automatic speech recognition errors.
The adversarial example generation method selects replacements using acoustic relations rather than uniform or unigram sampling from confusion sets.
Adversarial training with the proposed confusion-set-based examples improves neural machine translation robustness on hand‑crafted and real‑world noisy test sets compared to previous methods.
The proposed approach also yields improvements on clean test sets.
A two-stage training framework learns the frequency of semantic units in source text in an unsupervised manner.
During inference, the model selects sentences containing high-frequency semantic units to generate summaries.
The model outperforms other unsupervised methods on the CNN/Daily Mail summarization task.
The model achieves competitive ROUGE scores with far fewer parameters than several large-scale pre-trained models.
The model can be trained under low-resource language settings, enabling use where pre-trained models are not applicable.
Debiasing methods are evaluated across binary classification (Twitter sentiment), multi-class classification (profession prediction), and regression (valence prediction) tasks.
Data conditions, including target class imbalance and stereotyping, strongly influence the relative performance of debiasing methods.
Evaluating debiasing methods only on standard datasets does not allow reliable general conclusions about their efficacy.
NERDz is a manually annotated dataset of named entities for the Algerian vernacular dialect.
NERDz comprises parallel entity annotations in Latin, Arabic, and code-switched scripts.
A detailed overview of the NERDz annotations and inter-annotator agreement measures is provided.
Two preliminary baselines for NERDz are defined using a neural sequence labeling approach and an Algerian BERT model.
Annotation guidelines and the NERDz annotations are released for future work.
WAX is a large crowd-sourced dataset of English word associations with explanations and high-level relation type labels.
An analysis of the explanations provided in WAX is presented.
Several tasks are designed to probe the extent to which current pre-trained language models capture the underlying relations in WAX.
Current pre-trained language models struggle to capture the diversity of human associations, indicating that WAX is a rich benchmark for commonsense modeling and generation.

Randomly storing a uniform number of samples from the entire data stream achieves high performance in lifelong language learning for text classification and question answering, especially when memory size is low.
The impact of memory sampling strategies on model performance in lifelong language learning for text classification and question answering is systematically investigated.
BENEFICT integrates BERT, a multilayer perceptron, and a maximum subarray problem to derive contextualized review features, model user-item interactions, and generate explanations.
BENEFICT provides explanations for its recommendations, addressing the black-box nature of prior review-based recommender systems.
BENEFICT outperforms state-of-the-art recommender models.
Human judges assess that explanations generated by BENEFICT capture the essence of customer preferences and assist future customers in purchasing decisions.
BENEFICT is among the first recommender models to apply BERT within neural collaborative filtering.
Prepending learned embeddings to text queries, jointly trained with adversarial debiasing and a contrastive loss, serves as a debiasing technique for vision-language models.
The debiasing technique reduces multiple bias measures while causing minimal degradation to the image-text representation.
Ranking metrics are employed to assess bias in image-text representations.
BERTSeg is an unsupervised neural subword segmenter for neural machine translation that leverages contextualized semantic embeddings from characterBERT.
BERTSeg maximizes the generation probability of subword segmentations.
A generation probability-based regularization method enables BERTSeg to produce multiple segmentations for a single word, improving the robustness of neural machine translation.
BERTSeg with regularization achieves higher BLEU scores than BPE across multiple translation directions and datasets.
BERTSeg can be trained within a few minutes.
CSS combines self-training and self-supervised learning for few-shot dialogue state tracking.
CSS incorporates unlabeled dialogue state tracking data into self-training iterations using pseudo labels generated by a model pretrained on limited labeled data.
CSS employs a contrastive self-supervised method with dropout-based data augmentation to learn better representations for dialogue state tracking.
CSS achieves competitive performance in several few-shot scenarios on the MultiWOZ dataset.
Substituting part-of-speech tags for low-frequency words in sentences during continual training of masked language models yields a word‑tag‑hybrid BERT model.
The word‑tag‑hybrid BERT model improves performance on a sentence acceptability benchmark.
The word‑tag‑hybrid BERT model improves performance on a cross‑domain sentence acceptability evaluation corpus.
An annotated cross‑domain sentence acceptability evaluation corpus is released for future research.
A controlled equation generation solver uses control codes to guide reasoning logic and decode equation expressions derived from human references.
The controlled equation generation solver improves accuracy on the single-unknown Math23K benchmark.
The controlled equation generation solver improves accuracy on the multiple-unknown DRAW1K and HMWP benchmarks.
A method synthesizes conversational question-answer data covering open-ended, closed-ended, and unanswerable question types.
Separate generation flows are designed for each question type and combined within a single shared framework for CQA data synthesis.
A hierarchical answerability classification module improves the quality of synthetic data while enabling the generation of unanswerable questions.
Manual inspection shows that synthetic conversations exhibit characteristics similar to human-generated conversations.
CQA systems trained on the synthetic data achieve performance comparable to systems trained on human-annotated data across four domains.
A language model trained on concatenated source and target sentences can be used for neural machine translation.

The concatenation-based language model performs on par with an encoder-decoder Transformer for bilingual translation, translation with additional target monolingual data, and multilingual translation.
Findings suggest that an encoder-decoder architecture is redundant for neural machine translation.
Average Neuron-Wise Correlation (ANC) provides a straightforward alternative to CKA and CCA for measuring cross-lingual similarity.
ANC is exempt from the difficulties of CKA/CCA and is well-suited for cross-lingual contexts.
An empirical case study demonstrates that CKA/CCA indexes fail to capture valuable aspects of cross-lingual similarity relevant to zero-shot transfer.
ANC reveals that the "first align, then predict" pattern occurs in both masked and causal multilingual language models, including scaled versions up to 85 times the size of the original mBERT.
Cross-lingual transfer improves legal judgment prediction performance across languages.
Adapter-based fine-tuning enhances the benefit of cross-lingual transfer for legal judgment prediction.
Augmenting the training dataset with machine-translated documents increases model performance for legal judgment prediction.
Training a model across multiple legal domains yields better overall performance and improves worst-case scenarios compared to domain-specific training.
Training a model across multiple regions yields better overall performance and improves worst-case scenarios compared to region-specific training.
Incorporating Indian legal cases for cross-jurisdiction transfer further improves legal judgment prediction results.
Oversmoothing rate is defined to quantify the tendency of neural autoregressive sequence models to assign high probability to unreasonably short sequences.
A regularization term that explicitly minimizes the oversmoothing rate during training is proposed.
The regularization enables control of the oversmoothing rate by adjusting its strength.
Increasing the contribution of the oversmoothing loss reduces the probability and rank of the end-of-sequence token at inappropriate positions.
The regularization mitigates translation quality degradation with large beam sizes during beam search.
LED-Bert architecture enables localization via embodied dialog.
A pretraining strategy improves performance on localization via embodied dialog.
Graph-based scene representation is more effective than top-down 2D maps for localization via embodied dialog.
The presented approach outperforms previous baselines on localization via embodied dialog.
Several few-shot learning strategies are analyzed for cross-lingual sentiment analysis across three major model architectures.
Strategies for selecting source languages for transfer in cross-lingual few-shot learning are compared.
Linguistically similar and geographically similar languages are useful for cross-lingual adaptation in few-shot learning.
A mixture of random source languages provides more effective cross-lingual adaptation than selecting similar languages.
The encoder-only model XLM-R achieves the best downstream task performance among the evaluated architectures.
The EMOTyDA dataset is extended to include sentiment tags for each utterance.
A Dual Attention Mechanism (DAM) based multi-modal multi-task conversational framework is proposed for dialogue act classification, sentiment analysis, and emotion recognition.
The DAM module incorporates intra-modal and interactive inter-modal attentions with hierarchical multi-loss optimization to fuse modalities and learn generalized features across tasks.

A 2-step Deferred Optimisation Schedule (DOS) is introduced, comprising Meta-Net learning of an explicit weighting function and deferred re-weighting of a multi-task loss with a smaller learning rate.

Joint optimisation of multi-modal dialogue act classification, sentiment analysis, and emotion recognition tasks with the 2-step DOS and Meta-Net learning yields superior performance compared to alternative models and state-of-the-art approaches.
A two-step pipeline first extracts clinically relevant structured triples from radiology images and then generates free-text radiology reports conditioned on those triples.
The triple extraction component is supervised and produces (entity1, relation, entity2) triples from the image.
The report generation component conditions the generation of radiology reports on the extracted triples.
The pipeline achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MIMIC-CXR dataset according to standard text generation metrics.
The pipeline achieves state-of-the-art clinical accuracy on the MIMIC-CXR dataset as measured by recall, precision, and F1 using the CheXpert labeler.
The pipeline reduces the total number of errors and critical clinical errors in generated reports as assessed by expert human evaluation.
A large-scale perception survey of gender-based violence descriptions was conducted using automatically extracted Italian newspaper articles.
Regression models were trained to predict the salience of gender-based violence participants with respect to dimensions of perceived responsibility.
A fine-tuned BERT regression model predicts salient focus more accurately than salient blame and predicts perpetrators' salience more accurately than victims' salience.
Ridge regression models using linguistic-theory-based features achieve performance comparable to models using word-based features.
Linguistic choices in gender-based violence news reports are shown to influence perceived responsibility and can be modeled automatically.
Two new datasets are introduced for evaluating covariate shift robustness of open-world intent classification models.
Each dataset includes a cov-test set containing in-scope utterances sampled from unseen distributions together with out-of-scope utterances.
Evaluation of several open-world intent classifiers on the new datasets shows that models that perform well on the i.i.d. test set struggle to generalize to the cov-test set.
The new datasets provide a more realistic evaluation scenario for intent classification in task-oriented dialog systems.
A heterogeneous word-character text graph that combines word and character n-gram nodes with document nodes is introduced.
WCTextGCN is a graph-based neural model for the heterogeneous word-character text graph.
WCTextGAT is a graph-based neural model for the heterogeneous word-character text graph.
The introduced models consistently outperform competitive baselines and state-of-the-art graph-based models on text classification benchmarks.
The introduced models consistently outperform competitive baselines and state-of-the-art graph-based models on automatic text summarization benchmarks.
A corpus of human-written English medical queries and system responses is introduced.
The corpus includes crowdsourced and expert annotations for query seriousness and response risk types.
Aggregated crowdsourced labels agree with professional opinion more than individual crowdworkers for identifying medical queries and recognizing response risk types.
Classification experiments demonstrate that identifying medical query seriousness and response risk types can be automated, but errors may be severe.
BanglaParaphrase is a high-quality synthetic Bangla paraphrase dataset curated using a novel filtering pipeline.
The novel filtering pipeline preserves both semantics and diversity in the generated paraphrases.
BanglaParaphrase can be used to enhance other Bangla datasets.
Comparative analysis demonstrates that models trained on BanglaParaphrase achieve performance comparable to existing works, establishing the viability of the synthetic paraphrase generation pipeline.
A method combines an argumentation structure with a language model to generate natural argumentative dialogues.
The language model is trained to rewrite a proposition of an argumentation structure into the next utterance using keywords, stance, and contextual information.
Manual evaluation shows that the method improves the naturalness of generated dialogues while preserving argumentation consistency.
A tool for human evaluation of topic models has been developed.
TweetNER7 provides a Twitter NER dataset with seven entity types annotated on 11,382 tweets spanning September 2019 to August 2021, distributed to reflect temporal trends.
Baseline language models for Twitter NER are evaluated on TweetNER7.
Analysis reveals short-term degradation of NER model performance over time on Twitter data.
Fine-tuning language models on temporally segmented data mitigates performance degradation across different periods.
Self-labeling is demonstrated as an alternative approach when recent labeled Twitter data are unavailable.
TweetNER7 dataset and fine-tuned NER models are publicly released.
Gender and racial biases in BERT vary depending on the demographic groups represented in the training data.
Fine-tuning BERT on text authored by historically disadvantaged demographic groups is evaluated as a bias mitigation technique.
Fine-tuning BERT-like language models on text authored by certain demographic groups mitigates social biases against various target groups.
A deep reinforcement learning method controls sentence difficulty by rewarding the difference between output difficulty and target difficulty.
The method generates sentences of appropriate difficulty for a target audience.
The method outperforms existing approaches on English text simplification.
The method produces sentences whose grade-levels are closer to human references than previous studies, as estimated by a fine-tuned pre-trained model.
A replication study shows that local word order is crucial for natural language understanding performance across more than a hundred languages.
The study finds that global sentence structure has relatively little impact on natural language understanding performance in many languages.
The importance of local structure observed in English extends to the majority of languages examined, with some exceptions.
A method using vector space interpolation performs query set expansion for emerging topics.
The transformer model OPTIMUS, a variational autoencoder, is employed for vector space manipulation in query expansion.
Dirichlet interpolation is introduced as a query expansion technique and demonstrates promising performance.

The proposed methods generate new queries about sensitive topics by incorporating set-level diversity, which is not captured by traditional sentence-level augmentation methods such as paraphrasing or back-translation.
Pre-trained Sentence-BERT embeddings are employed to group samples with the same labels in embedding space for a legal document corpus within an active learning framework.
Calculated class embeddings are not close to their respective samples and do not partition the embedding space in a meaningful way.
Using class embeddings as an active learning strategy yields substantially lower performance than baseline active learning methods.
An experimental comparison of six transformer-based language models and human participants is conducted on argument hierarchy construction using the Chinese structure “NP1+BA/BEI+NP2+VP”.
Models and humans exhibit similar responses to (non-)agentive features in telic and atelic contexts.
Models demonstrate insufficient sensitivity to pragmatic functions that express undesirable events, unlike humans.
Models demonstrate insufficient sensitivity to individuation differences between common nouns and proper names, unlike humans.
Models tend to interpret the subject as an agent, whereas humans align agents independently of subject position in Mandarin Chinese.
A Neural Pairwise Ranking Model is applied to sentence-level readability assessment of Italian administrative texts.
Admin-It is a new parallel corpus of Italian administrative sentences simplified with three different rewriting strategies.
The Neural Pairwise Ranking Model is effective in zero-shot ranking of sentence pairs that include overall sentence-level simplifications.
Adding in-domain data enables the Neural Pairwise Ranking Model to achieve perfect ranking performance on sentence pairs from the same domain.
Characteristics of the training data have a larger impact than the amount of data on the model's readability ranking performance.
An evaluation methodology based on component combinations assesses combinational generalization of pre-trained language models in biased scenarios.
Pre-trained language models can overcome distribution shifts for specific tasks when trained with sufficient data.
Overfitting increases reliance on biases in pre-trained language models, thereby reducing their combinational generalization ability.
VLStereoSet is a vision-language probing dataset derived from StereoSet for measuring stereotypical bias in vision-language models.
A probing task is introduced that detects bias by evaluating a model’s tendency to select stereotypical statements as captions for anti-stereotypical images.
Metrics are defined to quantify a vision-language model’s overall stereotypical bias, intra-modal bias, and inter-modal bias.
Experiments on multiple representative pre-trained vision-language models reveal the presence of stereotypical biases across all bias categories, with gender bias being slightly more evident.
Analysis using gender bias data and two vision-language models indicates the existence of both intra-modal and inter-modal bias.
Detecting outliers in the training set and removing them before reweighting mitigates the impact of unlearnable examples on worst-group accuracy.
The outlier-aware training approach achieves competitive or better accuracy than Just Train Twice (JTT) on evaluated tasks.
The outlier-aware training approach can detect and remove annotation errors in the subset reweighted by JTT.
HieraDate provides three probing tasks—extraction, reasoning, and robustness—for evaluating date understanding in multi-hop reading comprehension.
HieraDate is constructed by enhancing HotpotQA and 2WikiMultiHopQA with multi-hop questions that involve date comparison and numerical reasoning.
Existing multi-hop models lack the ability to subtract two dates even when they perform well on date comparison and number subtraction tasks.
Probing questions from HieraDate improve performance on the main question-answering task.
HieraDate can be used for data augmentation to increase the robustness of multi-hop reading comprehension models.
An adaptive neural language model is used to estimate the information content of constructions and their containing utterances in English open-domain spoken dialogues.
Construction repetition lowers the information content of utterances in English open-domain spoken dialogues.
The information-reducing effect of construction usage increases as dialogues progress.
The magnitude of the information-reducing effect grows with repetition frequency and density.
Repetitions of referential constructions produce a stronger information-reducing effect than repetitions of other constructions.
A method for personalized emotional intensity estimation incorporates a writer’s personality test for Japanese SNS posts.
The method personalizes emotion analysis by using both text and the writer’s personality information.
Including personality information improves the performance of emotional intensity estimation.
A hybrid model that combines an existing personalized method with the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance.
Insightful analysis on pre-trained language models partially explains their exceptional downstream performance.
MiQA is a benchmark that assesses large language models' ability to reason with conventional metaphors.
MiQA combines metaphor detection and commonsense reasoning into a single inference task that requires selecting between literal and metaphorical registers.
Evaluation of state-of-the-art pre-trained models on MiQA binary-choice tasks reveals a large performance gap between small and very large models, ranging from chance to near-human level.
Analysis of the largest model on MiQA in a generative setting shows that approaching human performance requires careful multiple-shot prompting.
A dual-way neural dictionary retrieves words given definitions.
A dual-way neural dictionary generates definitions for queried words.
The model learns reverse dictionary and definition modeling tasks simultaneously in a multi-task fashion.
The model handles unknown words using embeddings.
The model maps words and definitions into a shared representation space via a shared layer.
The model attains strong automatic performance on prior benchmarks without additional resources.
Human annotators prefer the model’s outputs in both reference-less and reference-based evaluations.
Analysis indicates that employing multiple objectives improves learning.
A neural privacy-enhanced entity recognizer detects and classifies potential personal identifiers for text sanitization.
A privacy risk assessment measure based on span probabilities derived from a BERT language model is introduced.
A privacy risk assessment measure based on web search queries is introduced.
A privacy risk assessment measure based on a classifier trained on labeled data is introduced.
A linear optimization solver selects entities to mask to minimize semantic loss while keeping estimated privacy risk below a threshold.
Explicit control of the trade-off between privacy protection and data utility is enabled in text sanitization.
Evaluation is performed both with and without manually annotated data, demonstrating potential and identifying issues caused by specific types of personal data.
An empirical analysis of topic preservation in multi-document summarization is conducted on two datasets using eight summarization models.
Gold summaries in the Multi-News dataset exhibit higher topic distribution consistency than gold summaries in the Multi-XScience dataset.
Extractive multi-document summarization approaches preserve topic information from source documents better than abstractive approaches.
Linguistic features improve the performance of pre-trained language models on automatic readability assessment for long passages.
The performance gain from adding linguistic features to pre-trained language models diminishes when the dataset size exceeds 750 passages.
The Newsela dataset is unsuitable for automatic readability assessment.
A comprehensive analysis of linguistic disparity across world languages is performed.
Categorising languages solely by data availability is shown to be inaccurate.

The distribution of language data resources, NLP/CL research volume, inclusion in multilingual web platforms, and inclusion in pre‑trained multilingual models is analyzed using a speaker‑population and vitality based language categorisation.
Many languages are not covered by language data resources, multilingual web platforms, or pre‑trained multilingual models.
Substantial disparity exists among languages within the same language group.
The influence of language family, geographical location, GDP, and speaker population on linguistic disparity is examined.
Potential causes of linguistic disparity are identified and mitigation suggestions are proposed.
CLASP generates synthetic semantic parsing data with AlexaTM 20B to augment training of a 500M-parameter model.
